title: Scanline — a software 3D renderer
description: A real-time software 3D renderer built from scratch in TypeScript — no WebGL. Every triangle is transformed through model/view/projection, near-plane clipped in homogeneous space (Sutherland–Hodgman), perspective-divided, and scan-converted by an edge-function rasterizer with perspective-correct barycentric interpolation, a z-buffer and backface culling, then shaded per fragment with multi-light Blinn–Phong, procedural textures, ambient and distance fog — all writing pixels into a Uint32 framebuffer. Real-time shadow mapping renders scene depth from the key light through an auto-fit orthographic frustum and does a PCF shadow test per fragment. Eight debug views (shaded, albedo, wireframe, depth, normals, UV, overdraw heatmap, near-clip) make the GPU pipeline legible. Orbit camera, three scenes, live FPS/triangle/fill stats.
